summary: ATLAS Vision Systems (iter30) backend fully verified. All 25 unit + integration tests in test_iter30_vision_systems.py PASS both locally (127.0.0.1:8001) and via public ingress (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Covered: health/capabilities, camera+sensor registry (CRUD + kind filter), intrinsic + hand-eye calibration (with 404 on unknown device and camera.calibrated flag flip), synthetic frame ingest (deterministic checksum + 400 on missing payload), detection (synthetic_grid bbox bounds + synthetic_center deterministic + 404 on missing frame), IoU tracking (track ID persistence across frames), pose (pnp_stub residual≈0.5 with intrinsic; synthetic residual≈1.5), industrial inspection (defect→fail), PCB inspection (missing components→fail, sufficient→pass), fusion nms vs union, twin-link idempotency + mirror onto camera row, and full end-to-end pipeline. Non-destructive: /api/health, /api/knowledge-network/health, /api/membank/list, /api/dev/pipeline/status all return 200. No backend exceptions in supervisor logs.
critical_code_review_comments: Vision layer cleanly isolated: models/vision_models.py, services/vision.py, routes/vision.py — good separation of concerns.
Numpy-only implementation as promised: no OpenCV/torch imported, keeps container light.
Twin-link upsert is properly idempotent (verified) and mirrors twin_id back onto the device row — good pattern for reverse lookup.
seed_data_creation: Tests self-seed cameras/sensors/frames/detections per run; no persistent seed added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vision layer is stable at /api/vision/*. Local run: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter30_vision_systems.py`. Public ingress run: `ATLAS_BACKEND_URL=$REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL pytest backend/tests/test_iter30_vision_systems.py`. Non-vision endpoints (health/knowledge-network/membank/dev-pipeline) also green.
